question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Illinois and New York have similar populations.
options.B: The population gap between Illinois and New York might exceeds that between California and Ohio.
options.C: All states shown have very similar population levels.
options.D: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Illinois (12.3M) and New York (18.2M) have a 5.9M population difference, while California (5.5M) and Ohio (6.2M) have only a 0.7M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Illinois and New York into the same color bin (12-20M range), making their large difference appear similar, while California and Ohio appear more different due to different color bins.
